Quality Education.reason: Focus on writing and reading skills aligns with Target 4.4 ('increase relevant skills for employment').

Quality Education.reason: As a university course, it directly provides tertiary education (Target 4.3) and develops advanced literacy and critical thinking skills to 'write English papers' (Target 4.6).
evidence: write English papers on different topics and for different purposes

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The course uses literature to explore historical class inequality and aims to help students 'understand different cultural perspectives,' indirectly relating to understanding inequality (Target 10.2).
evidence: Excerpts from Elizabeth Gaskell’s novel Mary Barton (1848)

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: The course critically examines 'the experience of shopping' and how spaces 'influence people's attitudes towards consumption,' promoting awareness relevant to sustainable lifestyles (Target 12.8).
evidence: 購物的經驗 (the experience of shopping)
Thinking about shopping: William Kowinski’s essay ‘Kids in the Mall’
